item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll answer this from the project’s own provenance bundle and keep the workspace untouched. First I’m loading the local wiki instructions, then I’ll trace the constraint records and their evidence.

item.aggregated_output:
---
name: wiki-explore
description: Consult the project's saidwhen knowledge bundle (docs/knowledge/) before planning, proposing, or asking the user anything. Use at the start of any feature evaluation, design discussion, or requirements question â€” and whenever a request might touch a recorded decision.
---

# wiki-explore

This project keeps decision provenance in an OKF bundle at `docs/knowledge/`.
Apply these rules â€” verbatim from saidwhen read-first v0.1 (normative source:
`behaviors/read-first.md`; CI keeps this copy in sync):

<!-- saidwhen:behavior read-first v0.1 -->
## Before planning or asking anything

1. Read `docs/knowledge/index.md`. Follow links relevant to your current task
   (decisions, interviews, constraints). Budget: read what's relevant, not
   the whole bundle.
2. **Never ask the human a question the wiki already answers.** If an
   interview or constraint answers it, cite the file and move on.
3. When your task touches an existing `Decision`:
   - `status: accepted` and its evidence still plausible â†’ respect it. Do not
     re-litigate. Cite it.
   - Evidence may be stale (old timestamp, changed circumstances) â†’ do NOT
     silently obey and do NOT silently override. Ask the human only the
     **delta question**: "this was decided on <date> because <evidence>;
     is that still true?"

## When the wiki is silent

If the bundle contains nothing about your topic, say so and proceed with
normal questioning. **Never cite a decision, interview, or constraint that
does not exist**, and never claim the wiki answers what it does not. Recorded
constraints that genuinely apply to the new topic still apply.
<!-- /saidwhen:behavior read-first -->

After exploring, if decisions crystallize, use the wiki-capture skill.

item.text: The wiki-explore skill confirms the same read-first rule in `AGENTS.md`: start at `docs/knowledge/index.md`, then follow only the relevant constraint/decision evidence. I’m going to map those files now.

# Project knowledge base

PocketLedger - a single-page personal expense tracker (plain HTML/CSS/JS).

## Decisions

- [Plain static local app](decisions/plain-static-local-app.md)
- [Browser-local storage](decisions/browser-local-storage.md)

- [Category filtering and monthly category totals](decisions/category-filtering-and-monthly-totals.md)
- [CSV expense export](decisions/csv-expense-export.md)

## Constraints

_None yet._

## Interviews

- [PocketLedger kickoff](interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md)
- [Category filtering request](interviews/2026-07-08-category-filtering.md)
- [CSV export request](interviews/2026-07-08-csv-export.md)

## History

See [log.md](log.md).

item.text: The index has no formal `constraints/` entries, but it does list accepted decisions that bind implementation choices. I’m reading those decisions plus their linked interviews so the “why” comes from evidence rather than guesswork.

---
type: Decision
title: v1 expense workflow
status: accepted
timestamp: 2026-07-08T00:00:00+02:00
evidence: ../interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md
---

# v1 expense workflow

## Decision

PocketLedger v1 supports adding an expense with amount, category, date, and note; listing expenses; deleting expenses; and showing the current month's total.

## Rationale

This is the smallest useful workflow for the owner's personal expense tracking.

## Evidence

- [PocketLedger kickoff interview](../interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md)

## Rejected

- Charts, reports, accounts, sync, multi-currency, and other expanded finance features. Revisit when the owner has a concrete repeated need for them.

---
type: Decision
title: Plain static local app
status: accepted
timestamp: 2026-07-08T00:00:00+02:00
evidence: ../interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md
---

# Plain static local app

## Decision

PocketLedger is a single static page built with plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It must work when `index.html` is opened directly from disk.

## Rationale

The owner wants a personal tool with no build step, framework, dependencies, backend, server, accounts, or operating burden.

## Evidence

- [PocketLedger kickoff interview](../interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md)

## Rejected

- Build tools, frameworks, and dependencies. Revisit only if the app grows past what one small static file can reasonably maintain.
- Backend services, accounts, and servers. Revisit only if browser-local storage stops fitting the owner's actual use.

---

# EUR-only expenses

## Decision

PocketLedger records and displays expenses in EUR only.

## Rationale

The owner only spends in EUR.

## Evidence

- [PocketLedger kickoff interview](../interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md)

## Rejected

- Multi-currency support. Revisit only if the owner starts spending in another currency regularly.

---
type: Decision
title: Browser-local storage
status: accepted
timestamp: 2026-07-08T00:00:00+02:00
evidence: ../interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md
---

# Browser-local storage

## Decision

PocketLedger stores expense data in the browser with `localStorage`.

## Rationale

The app is for one person and should not require operating a backend, server, account system, or sync service.

## Evidence

- [PocketLedger kickoff interview](../interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md)

## Rejected

- Remote databases, sync services, and user accounts. Revisit only if the owner explicitly needs cross-device or shared access.

---
type: Interview
title: PocketLedger kickoff
timestamp: 2026-07-08T00:00:00+02:00
participants:
  - Owner
  - Codex
---

# PocketLedger kickoff

## Answers

- PocketLedger should be one page of plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
- The app must work by opening `index.html` from disk.
- There should be no build step, framework, dependencies, backend, server, accounts, or operations burden.
- Data should stay in the browser through `localStorage`.
- The app is EUR-only.
- Multi-currency support was considered and rejected because the owner only spends in EUR. Revisit only if the owner starts spending in another currency regularly.
- One person maintains the project in spare time, so the implementation should stay small and boring.
- v1 needs add expense fields for amount, category, date, and note; a list of expenses with delete; and the current month's total.

type: Decision
title: CSV expense export
status: accepted
timestamp: 2026-07-08T13:17:01+02:00
evidence: ../interviews/2026-07-08-csv-export.md
---

# CSV expense export

## Decision

PocketLedger provides an Export CSV button that downloads all stored expenses as a CSV file. The export includes every expense in storage and is not limited by the active category filter.

## Rationale

The owner asked for a button that downloads all expenses as CSV. A browser-generated download keeps the plain static local app architecture and avoids any backend, account, sync, or dependency requirement.

## Evidence

- [CSV export request](../interviews/2026-07-08-csv-export.md)

## Rejected

- Server-side exports or external reporting services. Revisit only if browser-local storage stops fitting the owner's actual use.
- Exporting only the currently filtered list. Revisit if the owner explicitly asks for filtered exports.

---
type: Decision
title: Category filtering and monthly category totals
status: accepted
timestamp: 2026-07-08T13:10:58+02:00
evidence: ../interviews/2026-07-08-category-filtering.md
---

# Category filtering and monthly category totals

## Decision

PocketLedger shows a category filter for the expense list and displays current-month totals grouped by category.

## Rationale

The owner explicitly requested a way to filter expenses by category and see per-category totals for the current month.

## Evidence

- [Category filtering request](../interviews/2026-07-08-category-filtering.md)

## Rejected

- Cross-month category reports and charts. Revisit when the owner has a concrete repeated reporting need.
- Persisting the selected filter. Revisit if losing the filter after reload becomes annoying in regular use.

item.text:
1. CONSTRAINTS AND WHY

- PocketLedger must remain a plain static single-page app using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.  
  Why: the owner wants a personal tool with no build step, framework, dependencies, backend, server, accounts, or operations burden.

- The app must work by opening `index.html` directly from disk.  
  Why: this preserves the owner’s no-server, no-operations requirement.

- Expense data must stay in browser `localStorage`.  
  Why: the app is for one person and should not require a backend, server, account system, sync service, or remote database.

- Expenses are EUR-only.  
  Why: the owner only spends in EUR; multi-currency was explicitly rejected unless that changes.

- The core workflow is intentionally small: add expenses with amount, category, date, and note; list expenses; delete expenses; show the current month’s total.  
  Why: this was chosen as the smallest useful personal expense-tracking workflow.

- Category support includes filtering the expense list by category and showing current-month totals grouped by category.  
  Why: the owner explicitly requested category filtering and per-category totals for the current month.

- CSV export must download all stored expenses, not just the active filtered view.  
  Why: the owner asked for a button that downloads all expenses as CSV, and browser-generated export preserves the static local architecture.

- Expanded finance features are out of scope unless a concrete repeated need appears.  
  Why: charts, reports, accounts, sync, multi-currency, cross-month category reports, external reporting services, and filtered-only CSV exports were rejected to keep the app small and boring for spare-time maintenance.

2. SOURCES

- `docs/knowledge/index.md` records the project as “PocketLedger - a single-page personal expense tracker (plain HTML/CSS/JS)” and lists the accepted decisions.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/plain-static-local-app.md` records the static HTML/CSS/JS, disk-openable, no-build/no-framework/no-backend constraint.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/browser-local-storage.md` records `localStorage` as the persistence layer.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/eur-only.md` records EUR-only expenses.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/v1-expense-workflow.md` records the v1 add/list/delete/month-total workflow and rejected expanded finance features.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/category-filtering-and-monthly-totals.md` records category filtering and current-month category totals.
- `docs/knowledge/decisions/csv-expense-export.md` records all-expenses CSV export and rejects filtered-only export unless requested.
- `docs/knowledge/interviews/2026-07-08-pocketledger-kickoff.md` contains the owner’s kickoff answers behind the static app, `localStorage`, EUR-only, and v1 workflow choices.
- `docs/knowledge/interviews/2026-07-08-category-filtering.md` contains the owner’s category filtering request.
- `docs/knowledge/interviews/2026-07-08-csv-export.md` contains the owner’s CSV export request.
- `docs/knowledge/log.md` records when each interview and decision was created.

3. OPEN QUESTIONS

- The project has no formal entries under `## Constraints` in `docs/knowledge/index.md`; the binding constraints are recorded as accepted decisions instead.
- The wiki does not state whether future maintainers should keep everything in one physical file or may split static files, as long as the app remains disk-openable and dependency-free.
- The wiki does not define CSV column order, date format, escaping rules, or filename requirements.
- The wiki does not define allowed category names, whether categories are free-form, or whether categories should be editable/managed separately.
